channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: EU tells Google to share search data, open Android to AI rivals
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The EU demands Google share search data and open Android to AI rivals under the Digital Markets Act. Google warns of privacy risks.
Read More: https://punchng.com/eu-tells-google-to-share-search-data-open-android-to-ai-rivals/

item.title: Don’t ignore lumps, bone pain, cancer expert tells Nigerians
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A cancer expert urges Nigerians not to ignore persistent lumps, swelling, or bone pain, as these could be sarcoma symptoms. Early detection is crucial for
Read More: https://punchng.com/dont-ignore-lumps-bone-pain-cancer-expert-tells-nigerians/

item.title: George captains unchanged England for Argentina clash
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Jamie George will captain England in an unchanged starting XV to face Argentina in the Nations Championship as players continue to avoid wearing national shirts in Buenos Aires.

item.title: Nigeria has no business being poor, says Shettima
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Vice President Kashim Shettima states Nigeria has no reason to remain poor, citing abundant resources and economic potential to overcome Nigeria poverty.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigeria-has-no-business-being-poor-says-shettima/

item.title: Scorecard: England vs India, second ODI, Cardiff
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Latest score from the second one-day international between England and India at Sophia Gardens. Watch live on Sky Sports Cricket.

item.description:

item.title: The Lehman Bros. moment of the AI bubble is coming, says this critic warning of fallout for tech stocks and the entire market

item.description: OpenAI is going down, and taking investors and an entire industry with it, argues artificial-intelligence critic Ed Zitron.

item.description: Rivers State Police Command has officially relaunched the Police Campaign Against Cultism and Other Vices (POCACOV). The post Rivers police relaunch POCACOV against deadly cult traps appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By John Alechenu, ABUJA The Chairman of the Senate Committee on Health, Secondary and Tertiary Services, Senator Ipalibo Banigo, has urged the Federal Government to channel revenue from the proposed Sugar-Sweetened Beverages (SSB) Tax to primary healthcare and screening programmes. Banigo made the c

item.title: APGA Leadership Reaffirms Support For Tinubu’s Second-Term Bid
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The national leadership of the All Progressives Grand Alliance (APGA) has reaffirmed its support for the re-election of President Bola Ahmed Tinubu in 2027, stating that the party has no presidential candidate of its own. This was disclosed at Umueze Anam in Anambra West Local Government Area during

item.title: Police Foil Planned Kidnapping in Kwara, Nab Two Foreign Nationals
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Kwara State Police Command has foiled a planned kidnapping and extortion attempt, arresting two nationals of the Republic of Togo and recovering the mobile phone allegedly used to threaten the intended victim. The Command said the operation followed a report lodged at the Okuta Police Division b

item.title: ‘I don’t want to sound callous’: Why base your Social Security claim on life expectancy? We could die at any time.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “The actual date of death ultimately affects only the surviving beneficiaries.”

item.title: Opposition’s Mockery Of Dr Alex Otti: The Forgotten Perspective
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Some months ago, certain comments aimed at Dr Alex Otti were made by opposition leaders and those comments seemed to plant a burden in my heart, a burden that stimulated my innate academic and scholarly approach to viewing critical issues. Commuting from Aba to Umuahia frequently, I begin to wonder

item.title: Why Nigeria’s spirits and wines industry is stepping up the fight against illicit trade
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigeria’s spirits and wines industry represents one of the country’s most significant consumer goods markets, contributing to manufacturing, logistics, retail, hospitality and government tax revenues. Yet a substantial share of the market continues to be undermined by illicit trade. IN a

item.title: Algeria President Mourns Children Killed In Algiers Orphanage Fire
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Algerian President Abdelmadjid Tebboune has expressed grief over the deaths of children in a fire that tore through a state-run child welfare home in the eastern suburbs of Algiers early on Thursday, killing at least 11 people and injuring 19 others. Tebboune said in a social media post that he had

item.title: Cameroon Cracks Down On Under-Reported Gold Production
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The government plans to step up controls at mining sites, introduce minimum gold delivery thresholds, improve monitoring of the various stages of ore recovery, and strengthen production traceability. item.title: Just in: Court orders forfeiture of N4.6bn jewellery, exotic cars linked to Achimugu
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: An Abuja High Court sitting in Apo has ordered the final forfeiture of assets valued at billions of naira linked to businesswoman, Ms Aisha Achimugu, to the Federal Government. The forfeited assets include jewellery worth N4.645 billion, 11 exotic vehicles valued at N4.293 billion, $50,000 cash, and

item.title: Fresh twist in David Umahi aide’s death as family files affidavit rejecting autopsy
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “While I welcome steps being taken to unravel the cause of my daughter’s death, my family and I will not accept any autopsy on my daughter’s body,” the affidavit stated.

item.title: Yamal Surpasses Mbappe As Youngest World Cup Finalist
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Lamine Yamal has overtaken Kylian Mbappe in the list of the youngest players to reach a FIFA World Cup final after helping Spain secure a 2-0 victory over France in the semi-finals. Goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro sent Spain into their first World Cup final since winning the tournament in

item.title: Argentina face action over Falklands banner
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Argentina face the prospect of disciplinary action from Fifa after their players celebrate the World Cup semi-final win against England with a banner in support of their country's claims to the Falkland Islands.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Mason Haynes, a veteran security professional best known for protecting the Kardashian-Jenner family and other global celebrities, has died at the age of 52 following a traffic accident. item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Amid the disruption caused by oil majors’ asset divestment, the Nigerian logistics provider is looking to drive its next phase of growth through its foray into electric ferries, regional expansion and global shipping partnerships.

item.description: Cirvee Academy has successfully hosted the third edition of Tech Cirvee Fest, bringing together more than 7,000 young people, entrepreneurs, technology professionals, and industry leaders for one of Africa's largest youth-focused technology festivals.

item.title: EFCC releases two Obi supporters after questioning over alleged naira abuse
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission has released two prominent members of the Obidient Movement, Peter Akah and Oruche Precious, popularly known as Mama Pee, after several hours of questioning. The post EFCC releases two Obi supporters after questioning over alleged naira abuse appeared fir

item.title: Abbott Boosts 2026 Profit Guidance as Sales Across Business Rise
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Abbott Laboratories raised its 2026 profit guidance after a stronger-than-expected second quarter driven by improved performance across most of its business lines.

item.title: NNL: Bayelsa United appoint Maikaba as new head coach
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Bayelsa United have appointed Abdu Maikaba as their new head coach, DAILY POST reports. The veteran tactician will combine the role with his duties as head coach of the Flying Eagles. The Prosperity Boys were relegated from the Nigeria Premier Football League, NPFL, last season. Maikaba has been tas

item.title: Court Orders Final Forfeiture Of Aisha Achimugu’s N4.6bn Jewellery, N4.3bn Exotic Cars
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: An FCT High Court in Apo, Abuja, has ordered the final forfeiture of billions of naira worth of assets linked to businesswoman Aisha Achimugu to the Federal Government. The post Court Orders Final Forfeiture Of Aisha Achimugu’s N4.6bn Jewellery, N4.3bn Exotic Cars appeared first on Trending Ne

item.title: Venezuela’s recovery clouded by uncertainty three weeks after earthquake
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: In Venezuela, the focus has shifted from rescue to rebuilding three weeks after devastating earthquakes.

item.title: Troops rescue 13 kidnap victims, foil terrorists’ operations in Zamfara
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Idris Salisu, Gusau Troops of the Joint Task Force North West, Operation FANSAN YAMMA, have rescued 13 kidnapped victims during separate intelligence-led operations in Anka and Kaura Namoda Local Government Areas of Zamfara State. The development was disclosed in a statement issued in Gusau by th

item.title: 11 killed in Abuja–Kaduna highway crash — FRSC
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The Federal Road Safety Corps said a speeding commercial bus carrying 16 passengers rammed into a stationary truck on the Abuja–Kaduna Expressway. The post 11 killed in Abuja–Kaduna highway crash — FRSC appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: US lawmakers back total aid freeze for Nigeria over insecurity
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The United States House of Representatives has approved an amendment seeking to suspend all American assistance to Nigeria until the Federal Government demonstrates measurable progress in tackling insecurity. The post US lawmakers back total aid freeze for Nigeria over insecurity appeared first on T

item.title: Basketmouth Pays Tribute To His Late Parents, Sound Sultan, Kayode Peters
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Popular comedian cum actor, Bright Okpocha, known as Basketmouth, has paid tribute to his late family members and celebrity friends. ‎He shared a comic video on his Instagram and X, taking a swipe at the government. He said that if he had invested the 27 years he spent in his comedy career in sellin

item.title: ‘Destroyed my whole life’: Lebanese families wiped out in Israeli attacks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Hussein Saleh describes an Israeli air attack in Tyre that killed his family, including his wife and his only child.

item.title: Osun: Lions Clubs Promote Environmental Hygiene, Donate Sanitation Materials
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Osun New Era Lions Club and Igbajo Achievers Lions Club on Thursday organised a sanitation campaign at Farida Market, Olorunsogo, Dada Estate, Osogbo, as part of efforts to promote environmental cleanliness and improve public health. The initiative featured a market-wide clean-up exercise, envir

item.title: NEPC Unlocks N1bn For 128 Women-Owned Businesses
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Nigerian Export Promotion Council (NEPC) has successfully facilitated the disbursement of grants to 128 women-owned export-oriented Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) under the Women Exporters in the Digital Economy (WEIDE) Fund, a landmark initiative designed to empower women entrepren
